title: MT loop-control checkpoint contract (bounded retries + verifier feedback)
goal: Implement the bounded mailbox-loop-control checkpoint contract that lets Role Mailbox coordinate Micro-Task retries, verifier feedback, verification-needed waits, escalation, and completion reports through structured checkpoints instead of transcript replay. Folds WP-1-Role-Mailbox-Micro-Task-Loop-Control-v1 stub intent: small local models, cloud models, reviewers, and operators can resume or inspect MT loops from compact state.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/mt_loop_control.rs with `MtLoopCheckpoint`, `MtLoopState`, `MtLoopControlBudget` types + `MtLoopControl` orchestration helper; integration tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/mt_loop_control_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-179 micro_task_* message families provide the typed feedback payloads; MT-184 MicroTaskJob provides the state container.
implementation_notes: Per spec line 6147 + folded stub WP-1-Role-Mailbox-Micro-Task-Loop-Control-v1 intent (refinement.json line 987): 'durable loop-control contract so small local models, cloud models, reviewers, and operators can resume or inspect Micro-Task loops from compact state rather than chat chronology.' Define `MtLoopCheckpoint { checkpoint_id: Uuid v7, job_id: Uuid v7 (FK MicroTaskJob), iteration_n: u32, state_at_checkpoint: MtLoopState, retry_budget_remaining: u32, verifier_feedback_history: Vec<VerifierFeedbackRef>, compact_summary: String (<= 2048 bytes — what next executor needs to continue), evidence_pointers: Vec<EvidencePointer>, created_at_utc, created_by_session: Uuid v7 }`. `MtLoopState` enum: `WaitingForVerifier`, `IteratingOnFeedback { feedback_id }`, `VerificationNeeded { reason }`, `EscalationProposed { target_tier }`, `Completed { signal: CompletionSignal }`. `MtLoopControlBudget { max_iterations: u32, max_consecutive_failures: u32, max_hardgate_pause_secs: u32, max_verifier_feedback_loops: u32 }` loaded from MT contract or default. `MtLoopControl::record_checkpoint(job, state, summary, evidence) -> MtLoopCheckpoint` writes to Postgres with FK to MicroTaskJob, enforces budget (returns LoopBudgetExceeded if violated). `MtLoopControl::resume_from(checkpoint_id) -> ResumeContext` produces the compact context a fresh executor needs (no transcript replay required). Spec line 6147 invariant enforced: must NOT authorize open-ended 'keep trying' loops; budget exhaustion returns LoopBudgetExceeded which forces escalation (handed to MT-188). Tests: (a) record sequential checkpoints, resume from last — get exact compact context; (b) exceed max_iterations, expect LoopBudgetExceeded; (c) resume across executor handoff (session_id change), checkpoint chain queryable; (d) verifier_feedback_history append-only (no rewrites).
risk_if_missed: Without compact checkpoints, MT loops require transcript replay to resume — silently violating the folded stub's 'compact state rather than chat chronology' invariant. Open-ended 'keep trying' loops become possible because the bounded-retry contract has no enforcement point.
validator_focus: Budget enforcement returns typed error (not silent continuation); compact_summary size bounded (CHECK constraint or runtime assertion); resume_from produces same context regardless of which executor calls it (determinism).

minimum_controls: Budget exhaustion test asserts LoopBudgetExceeded forces escalation (no silent continuation).
Compact summary CHECK constraint enforces <= 2048 bytes at the database level.
Resume determinism proven by cross-executor test: two different session_ids resuming from same checkpoint produce byte-identical ResumeContext.
